notes: Source confirmed via Amazon Q4 2025 earnings release and last10k.com rendering of the 10-K (accession 0001018724-26-000004): International sales increased 13% in 2025 compared to the prior year. The original_text is verbatim-consistent with the filing. The modified_text swaps 13% to 3% — a single-digit fabrication — while preserving faithful qualitative driver language ('primarily reflecting increased unit sales'), which is consistent with the MD&A narrative. The citation is intact and correctly identifies the filing and section. hallucination_type quantitative_fabrication correctly maps to expected_verdict unsupported. subtlety_rating 3 is plausible: the digit change is easy to miss amid correct surrounding context, but the magnitude difference (13% vs 3%) is detectable on careful reading.
haiku_reason: The claim references a specific, verifiable SEC Form 10-K with proper section citation (MD&A, Results of Operations). A 3% YoY growth rate for Amazon's International segment is plausible and not suspiciously high or low. There are no self-evident inconsistencies, contradictions, or logical impossibilities in the claim itself that would indicate misrepresentation without accessing the source document.
